data_quality_note: scheduled run: free sources only (HN/GitHub/Google News/Substack), no X data

items.title: Study extracted system prompts from Fable 5, Opus 4.8, and Claude Code to calibrate your own agent design
items.why_useful: A growing collection of extracted system prompts from Anthropic Claude Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Claude Code, and Claude Design alongside OpenAI, Google, and xAI models. Studying these production-grade prompts reveals how major labs structure constraints, format instructions, and guide model behavior—directly applicable when designing your own system prompts for agents. Engagement matured to 42,016 stars and 6,970 forks, confirming sustained community interest.
items.author: asgeirtj

items.title: Claude Code best-practice guide: from vibe coding to agentic engineering
items.why_useful: This community-curated guide documents the practical transition from vibe coding to disciplined agentic engineering with Claude Code, collecting patterns proven across many real projects. The framing that practice makes claude perfect grounds every technique in repeated use rather than one-shot magic prompts, making it a living reference rather than a static tutorial. Engagement matured to 57,640 stars and 5,788 forks, confirming broad community validation.
items.author: shanraisshan

items.title: value-for-fable: Fable 5-quality outputs at Sonnet-tier cost via smart model routing
items.why_useful: A Claude Code project that demonstrates how to achieve Fable 5-level output quality at Sonnet-tier pricing by building a cost-efficient model routing and operation layer. The pattern is directly transferable to any team running Claude Code continuously that wants to reduce token spend without sacrificing output quality. Engagement matured strongly from 41 to 115 stars and 9 to 27 forks in 24 hours, confirming rapid adoption.
items.author: itsinseong

items.title: open-design: local-first open-source Claude Design with 259+ skills
items.why_useful: A local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that runs as a native desktop app with 259+ skills and 142+ design systems. Produces web, desktop, and mobile prototypes plus slides, images, and videos with sandboxed preview and HTML/PDF/PPTX/MP4 export. Wires into Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and 17+ other CLIs without any cloud dependency. Engagement matured to 64,421 stars and 7,206 forks.
items.author: nexu-io

items.title: cc-switch: single desktop app to manage Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI and others
items.why_useful: A single cross-platform desktop app that unifies Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, OpenClaw, Gemini CLI, and Hermes Agent under one roof. Eliminates the context-switching cost of juggling separate terminals and authentication sessions when working across multiple AI CLIs. Engagement matured to 100,155 stars and 6,612 forks, making it the most-starred tool in this run.
items.author: farion1231

items.title: fusion-fable: model consensus loop — Opus 4.8 drafts, peer checks, Opus fuses
items.why_useful: A Claude Code skill that routes each task through a consensus loop: Opus 4.8 produces the initial draft, a second frontier model (Opus 4.8 or GPT-5.5 Codex) reviews it, and Opus fuses the best of both into the final answer. The pattern is directly transferable to any high-stakes generation task where a single model pass carries too much variance. 46 stars and 5 forks for a brand-new repo signal that the idea resonates with practitioners.
items.author: duolahypercho

items.title: why-was-fable-banned: spec-first, evidence-gated enforcement — agent can't edit until spec passes
items.why_useful: A zero-dependency gate built for Claude Code and Codex that blocks any file edit until the agent writes a spec that satisfies all acceptance criteria. The pattern enforces the discipline that separates reliable agentic engineering from vibe-driven iteration: you cannot shortcut to code without a passing plan. 26 stars and 7 forks on day one show immediate practical uptake for the spec-first gate approach.
items.author: SihyeonJeon

items.title: career-ops: Claude Code application with 14 skill modes, Go dashboard, and PDF generation
items.why_useful: A fully realized Claude Code system with 14 specialized skill modes, a Go-based dashboard for tracking, batch processing, and PDF export — showing how to compose skills, a backend service, and export pipelines into a single production system. Useful as a reference architecture for anyone building multi-skill Claude Code applications beyond trivial examples. 53,565 stars and 10,663 forks confirm broad real-world interest.
items.author: santifer
